project_type: Python Strands Agents SDK project deployable to Bedrock AgentCore
build_system: uv for dependency management, Docker for container builds
verification_instructions: 1. Run uv sync to install dependencies. 2. Run uv run pytest tests/ -v to run tests. 3. Run uv run ruff check . to lint. 4. Optionally run uv run python -c 'from agent import app; print(app)' to verify import.
setup_instructions: 1. cd /projects/sandbox/kiro-autonomous-agent-demo. 2. uv init --python 3.11 (if not already done). 3. uv add strands-agents strands-agents-tools bedrock-agentcore fastapi 'uvicorn[standard]' pydantic httpx. 4. uv add --dev pytest ruff.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode. Python 3.11 via pyenv. uv, pytest, ruff available. No AWS credentials available so agent cannot actually be invoked end-to-end, but code structure and local HTTP server testing are feasible.
contribution_requirements: MIT license. Repo is essentially empty so we are greenfield.
key_patterns: Strands SDK: Agent() class with tools parameter, @tool decorator for custom tools. AgentCore: Two deployment paths - (A) SDK Integration using bedrock-agentcore with BedrockAgentCoreApp + @app.entrypoint decorator, or (B) Custom FastAPI with /invocations POST and /ping GET endpoints. Both require ARM64 Docker containers on port 8080. The SDK path is simpler; Custom path gives full control. We will implement BOTH approaches as examples.
relevant_files: agent.py (to be created - main agent code with custom tools)
agent_agentcore_sdk.py (to be created - SDK integration deployment entrypoint)
agent_agentcore_custom.py (to be created - Custom FastAPI deployment entrypoint)
tools/ (to be created - custom tools directory)
pyproject.toml (to be created by uv init)
Dockerfile (to be created - ARM64 container for AgentCore)
deploy/deploy_agent.py (to be created - boto3 deployment script)
deploy/invoke_agent.py (to be created - boto3 invocation script)
directory_structure: Root: agent code, config, Dockerfile. tools/: custom tool implementations. deploy/: deployment and invocation scripts. tests/: pytest unit tests.
